title: Bachelor of Commerce (Honours with research) Applied Finance and Analytics
content: The 4-year BCom (Honours with Research) in Applied Finance and Analytics is an undergraduate degree program that combines finance, analytics, and research to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of the financial industry. The program is designed to equip students with a deep understanding of finance principles, analytical tools, and research methodologies, which are highly valued in the financial industry. The curriculum typically covers subjects such as financial accounting, corporate finance, investment analysis, risk management, financial modeling, data analysis, statistics, econometrics, and research methodology. Students also develop skills in programming languages such as R, SPSS, and EViews, and data visualization tools such as Tableau and also build a career in research through quality publishing research articles in Indexed Journals. The program's unique feature is that it provides students with an opportunity to conduct independent research projects in the field of finance and analytics, which enables them to gain practical knowledge and skills in the industry. The research projects typically involve collecting and analyzing data related to financial markets, companies, and industries and developing models and strategies to improve financial performance. Graduates with a BCom (Honours with Research) in Applied Finance and Analytics can pursue careers in a range of industries, including banking, KPOs, investment management, insurance, consulting, and research. Job roles may include financial analyst, investment banker, risk analyst, data analyst, financial planner, business consultant, or research analyst. Additionally, students may also choose to pursue higher education in the field, such as a Master of Finance, Master of Business Analytics, or a Ph.D. in Finance or Economics, to further specialize their skills and knowledge and advance their careers. XII with minimum 50% marks aggregate and Mathematics/Commerce as a subject in XII level. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list For Migration from Other Universities: 1) B.Com (Hons/Hons with Research) Ist yr. minimum 50% marks or equivalent 2) 50% or above in best four relevant subjects at XII level 01 Apply the knowledge of Accounting and Finance to solve real life business issues. 02 Apply knowledge related to the field of finance in project management as an entrepreneur/a team member/a team leader to manage projects in multidisciplinary environments. 03 Analyze and interpret financial and non-financial information for Business Decision making 04 Explore alternate solutions for financial issues and develop systems and processes that meet the specified needs of business with appropriate consideration for social, cultural, economic and financial issues. 05 Select, and apply appropriate modern management & IT tools, techniques and resources including prediction & modelling to financial activities. 06 Apply the contextual knowledge to assess the issues related to society, health, safety, law, and culture pertaining to business management practices. 07 Apprehend and visualize the impact of financial decisions in societal and environmental context and demonstrate the knowledge of sustainable growth and development. 08 Imbibe professional ethics related to financial management practices. 09 Communicate effectively with business community and with society at large. 10 Explore business problems from an entrepreneurial perspective 01 Graduates will be able to demonstrate competency and leadership to become professional, employees and entrepreneurs leading to a successful career. 02 Graduates can pursue a relevant post-graduate study such as MBA, MBE, M.Com, MFC, CA, CS, ICWA and other professional courses. 03 Graduates will pursue lifelong learning in generating innovative solutions and practices using research and complex problem-solving skills. 01 Student will learn relevant finance career skills, applying both Quantitative and Qualitative Knowledge to their future career in business. 02 Student will be able to prove proficiency with the ability to engage in competitive exams like CA,CS, CMA and other courses. 03 Apply Descriptive, Predictive and Prescriptive analytical tools for business problem solving. XII with minimum 50% marks aggregate and Mathematics/Commerce as a subject in XII level. For Migration from Other Universities: 1) B.Com (Hons/Hons with Research) Ist yr. minimum 50% marks or equivalent 2) 50% or above in best four relevant subjects at XII level
